summary: Small multi-system decomp matching PR that finished 9 functions and improved 11 more across Kirby specials, ground/stage code, item boomerang code, sound test, random stage switch, fighter collision, and memory-card helpers. Evidence includes a bot report showing +2064 matched-code bytes and 9 new 100% matches, plus minimal human discussion accepting possible small/noisy gains.

key_files.role: Replaced direct floor-collision helper call with Kirby special helper in ftKb_SpecialN_800F598C; bot report says this function became a new 100% match.
Adjusted declaration order and added PAD_STACK(24) in two Mewtwo-copy Kirby special end animation functions; bot report shows improvements but not full matches for these PAD_STACK edits.

Reworked stack/register shaping in Zelda-copy Kirby special loop/collision functions, including PAD_STACK and changing ftCo_80096900 float argument from 0.0F to 1.0F in two collision functions; two collision functions became new 100% matches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Switched DmgLogEntry discriminator access from entry->kind to entry->x0 in ftColl_8007BBCC; bot report shows a very small unmatched-item improvement.
Changed gm_80164330 argument type to s32, added explicit u8/u16 casts for table index and bit shifts, and preserved OSReport path; bot report shows improvement from 96.41% to 98.28%.
Updated gm_80164330 prototype from u8 to s32 to match the body change.
Changed ground object access and jobj traversal shape in grBigBlueRoute_8020C238, with PAD_STACK(8); bot report says this function became a new 100% match.

Introduced static sbss HSD_GObj* grPu_804D6AA0, stored Ground_801C49F8 result, and simplified parentheses in camera-bound expression; bot report shows function and .sbss improvements, not a full function match.
Added placeholder.h include, PAD_STACK(8), and removed gobj->user_data = NULL in un_80301634; bot report says this function became a new 100% match.
Adjusted inline norm_xF74 by changing angle to f64 and flipping facing_dir branch condition; bot report says two boomerang functions became new 100% matches and other item functions improved.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Changed lb_8001B760 to return int, converted repeated CARD_RESULT_BUSY polling loops to assignment-in-condition do/while form, and adjusted one local result type to s32; bot report says lb_8001B760 and lb_8001B8C8 became new 100% matches.
Updated lb_8001B760 prototype from void to int to match body recovery.

what_changed: Matched-code report improved by +0.05% / +2064 bytes, with 9 new 100% matches listed by decomp-dev bot.

Kirby special files received helper-call, declaration-order, PAD_STACK, pointer/local, and literal-argument changes; ftKb_SpecialN_800F598C, ftKb_SkSpecialNCancel_Coll, and ftKb_SkSpecialNEnd_Coll reached 100%.
lbcardnew card-result polling loops were compacted into do/while assignment conditions; lb_8001B760 was recovered as returning int and the header was updated.
gm_80164330 signature changed from u8 to s32 in both source and header, with explicit casts at narrower use sites.
grBigBlueRoute_8020C238 was reshaped around gobj->hsd_obj, gobj->user_data, redundant null-check style, and PAD_STACK to reach 100%.
soundtest un_80301634 added a placeholder include and PAD_STACK while removing an explicit user_data NULL assignment, reaching 100%.
itlinkboomerang inline angle normalization changed f32 to f64 and inverted the facing_dir condition; two item functions reached 100%.
grpura introduced static sbss storage for Ground_801C49F8 result, improving both grPura_80211D00 and .sbss per bot report.

smart_moves.text: Recovered function signatures in both source and owning headers for gm_80164330 and lb_8001B760 instead of leaving body/header disagreement.
Used a recurring source-level do/while assignment condition for repeated lbcardnew busy-result polling, reducing copied loop bodies while matching several card helper functions.
Kept small tactical changes tied to bot-reported score movement and accepted them after explicit discussion about possible noise/fake matches.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves a function returns a value, update the function and its header prototype together; PR evidence shows lb_8001B760 changed from void to int in both source and header.
Repeated polling loops may match better as do/while loops with assignment in the loop condition when the result is repeatedly refreshed until it is no longer 0xB.

Argument-width recovery can require a wider public signature plus explicit casts at narrower table-index or bit-shift use sites, as shown by gm_80164330 changing to s32 while indexing with (u8) and shifting with (u16).

For small matching gains, separate strong new 100% matches from weak score-only improvements; this PR's author explicitly offered to revert smaller gains as possible noise/fake matches, and reviewer response only says they were fine.

naming_conventions.name: gp
naming_conventions.meaning: Ground* local from gobj->user_data in ground route code

naming_conventions.name: ip
naming_conventions.meaning: Item* local/parameter in item helper code

naming_conventions.name: grPu_804D6AA0
naming_conventions.meaning: Address-style static HSD_GObj* introduced for grpura sbss; semantic role is not established by PR text.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: PAD_STACK(24), PAD_STACK(16), and PAD_STACK(8) were added in several functions to affect stack layout; bot report supports some resulting 100% matches and some smaller improvements, but no per-function objdiff details beyond the bot table are loaded.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration order and local lifetime shaping
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Moved u32* charge before Fighter* fp in ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim and initialized a retained Fighter* new_var = fp in ftKb_SkSpecialAirNLoop_Anim.
Changed local and signature types, including gm_80164330(u8) to gm_80164330(s32), bit-shift casts to u16, lbcardnew result to s32 in one function, and boomerang angle from f32 to f64.
Converted repeated card-result loops into assignment-in-condition do/while loops and inverted boomerang facing_dir branch condition while preserving the same two angle expressions.
Added static HSD_GObj* grPu_804D6AA0 and assigned Ground_801C49F8() to it; bot report separately notes .sbss improvement from 0.00% to 50.00%.

review_feedback.author: malvarezcastillo
review_feedback.text: I can revert the smaller gains if they're just noise / fake matches
review_feedback.interpretation: Author flagged uncertainty around smaller score improvements and offered revert if reviewers considered them noise or fake matches.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.interpretation: Reviewer/commenter accepted the small gains in this PR context; no detailed rationale or per-change approval intent is available.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching lb_8001B760 or repeated lb_8001B6F8 busy polling loops to see whether assignment-in-condition do/while became stable style.
Search for PAD_STACK additions in ftKb_SpecialNYs and ftKb_SpecialNZd to distinguish accepted stack padding from later cleanup.
Search gm_80164330 call sites after this PR to confirm whether s32 signature remained and whether callers pass wider stage indices.
Search grPu_804D6AA0 and grpura .sbss changes to verify whether the new static storage persisted or was later renamed/removed.
Search soundtest placeholder.h include history around un_80301634 to determine whether placeholder include was temporary matching scaffolding.

notes: Strong evidence for changed files, exact source edits, and bot-reported matching deltas.
PR body is empty and there are no review comments or detailed reviewer explanations.
Bot report provides match percentages and new matches but not local objdiff command output or per-edit reasoning.

Some edits are tactical and potentially noisy, especially PAD_STACK, placeholder.h include, .sbss storage, and very small unmatched-item improvements; this uncertainty is reflected rather than promoted as a global standard.
The reviewer statement 'It's fine.' is brief; it supports acceptance of the PR discussion but not a broad rule that small gains or fake-looking changes are always acceptable.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes should move with recovered function return/signature evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2225 changed lb_8001B760 from void to int in both lbcardnew.c and lbcardnew.h, and changed gm_80164330 from u8 to s32 in both gm_1601.c and gm_1601.h. This is a concrete example of synchronizing owning headers when matching work proves a signature.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do/while assignment condition can express repeated CARD busy polling
accepted_candidate_records.text: Several lbcardnew helpers replaced a two-line body that assigns result then checks result == 0xB with `while ((result = lb_8001B6F8()) == 0xB);`. The bot report lists new 100% matches for lb_8001B760 and lb_8001B8C8.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Small score-only gains should be tracked separately from full matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2225 contains both 9 new 100% matches and smaller unmatched-item improvements. The author explicitly offered to revert smaller gains if they were noise/fake matches, while reviewer response was only 'It's fine.' This supports recording uncertainty for score-only tactical edits.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify that bot score tables are useful but not a substitute for per-edit tactic justification
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2225 shows PAD_STACK, placeholder include, declaration order, and sbss edits backed by a decomp-dev bot score table and brief reviewer acceptance. Curator may consider adding guidance that bot-reported new 100% matches support a PR intake record, but broad tactical patterns still need narrow objdiff/build evidence before becoming reusable standards.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already warn that PAD_STACK and similar tactics need evidence; this PR is a useful historical example but lacks detailed verifier logs beyond the bot report.

source_update_candidates.title: lbcardnew uses CARD busy polling helper lb_8001B6F8 in multiple save/load helpers
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2225 repeatedly changes lbcardnew helpers to poll `lb_8001B6F8()` while result remains 0xB, including lb_8001B760, lb_8001B7E0, lb_8001B8C8, lb_8001BA44, lb_8001BC18, lb_8001BD34, lb_8001BFD8, and lb_8001C2D8.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future matching searches in the memory-card subsystem, but should be checked against current source before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not promote PAD_STACK sizes from this PR as a general rule; they are per-function tactics with only bot report support in the loaded slice.
Do not infer that placeholder.h is a preferred include for soundtest; the diff only shows it was added in a matching PR.
Do not infer semantic meaning for grPu_804D6AA0 beyond address-style static HSD_GObj storage introduced in grpura.
Do not claim all modified functions became 100% matches; the bot report separates 9 new matches from 11 unmatched-item improvements.
